methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series A → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.0x) to $34M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 3 same-sector + same-stage peers in Defense & Security. Range: $150M - $500M.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Defense & Dual-use

private.name: Stark Defence
private.rationale: Same sector (Defense & Security), same stage (Series A), post-money $500M

private.name: Vatn Systems
private.rationale: Same sector (Defense & Security), same stage (Series A), post-money $300M

private.name: Ares Industries
private.rationale: Same sector (Defense & Security), same stage (Series A), post-money $150M

data.narrative:
Terrahaptix is a Series A-stage defense and security company founded by Nathan Nwachuku and Maxwell Maduka that has raised $34M to date. The valuation analysis draws on a peer universe of 10 comparable companies—2 public (DRSHF, IDEAFORGE.NS) and 8 private—operating in the same sector and stage, including Stark Defence, Vatn Systems, and Ares Industries.

Two triangulation methods were applied. Stage-progression analysis uses the $34M raised as a valuation proxy and applies a 2.0x step-up multiplier typical of Series A advancement, yielding $68M. Peer-median analysis examines three same-stage, same-sector comparables with a median valuation of $300M and a range spanning $150M to $500M, implying $300M. The peer set includes Stark Defence at $500M, Vatn Systems at $300M, and Ares Industries at $150M.

The two-method triangulation produces a valuation range of $58M (low) to $345M (high), with a median of $184M. The wide spread reflects material uncertainty: stage-progression anchors to a conservative capital-raised baseline, while peer-median reflects heterogeneity in defense-sector Series A valuations. Key uncertainties include relative technology differentiation versus peers, customer concentration and contract durability, and regulatory/export compliance risk inherent to the defense sector. The $184M median represents the midpoint between methodologies but should be weighted by confidence in peer comparability.
